cmd:
python3 << 'EOF'
# Simulating the buggy code
per_page = 10

# Page 1: offset = (1-1)*10 - (1 if 1>1 else 0) = 0 - 0 = 0
page1_offset = (1 - 1) * per_page - (1 if 1 > 1 else 0)
print(f"Page 1: offset = {page1_offset}, items from {page1_offset} to {page1_offset + per_page}")

# Page 2: offset = (2-1)*10 - (1 if 2>1 else 0) = 10 - 1 = 9
page2_offset = (2 - 1) * per_page - (1 if 2 > 1 else 0)
print(f"Page 2: offset = {page2_offset}, items from {page2_offset} to {page2_offset + per_page}")

print("\n
output_preview:
Page 1: offset = 0, items from 0 to 10
Page 2: offset = 9, items from 9 to 19

The bug: Page 1 gets items 0-9 (IDs 1-10), but Page 2 starts at 9 (ID 10)
Item with ID 10 appears on both pages!

Correct calculation should be:
Page 1: offset = 0, items from 0 to 10 (IDs 1-10)
Page 2: offset = 10, items from 10 to 20 (IDs 11-20)
